creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi Ralf, thanks for the update including QA. 

A few administrative tips:

Feature updates (unless they're also security updates) are always "Affects Only Me" in the sense that it "affects the maintainer", who aims to update the port, the class of the issue.

Build logs are not required, unless they are requested by someone first. Instead you can confirm QA in your comment as follows:

portlint: OK (looks fine.)
testport: OK (poudriere: <versions>, <archs>, <OPTIONS> tested)

If you can provide a changelog URL for version updates, please do so in the URL: field, pointing to an upstream URL that is a permalink (ie; its contents doesn't change over time) to the changes for that version. If it includes previous versions too that's fine.

When providing logs, or anything else which is a large body of text/data, use attachments instead of external URL's, which can become stale or go missing entirely over time.

It's better if all information is completely self-contained and available in the issue.
